characters.physical_details: Six-one, stocky, ruddy-faced, receding hairline, front-tooth gap; careless expensive casual clothes.

key_relationships.paul_decker: Best friend since college; Martin was Paul's best man.
key_relationships.tommy_renaldi: Protected Tommy's role in the 2002 Ray Toomey fire for twenty-two years; left him the lake house.
key_relationships.joel_beckman: Former fraternity friend; had an affair with Joel's ex-wife Karen.
key_relationships.cliff_haywood: Owed Cliff $40,000 from the failed Roseville duplex deal.
key_relationships.nate_griggs: Longtime league friend; indulged Nate's recordings and voicemail bits.
key_relationships.doug_whitfield: Noticed Doug's decline and pushed him to come after a July fridge call.

characters.occupation_status: Self-employed financial consultant; Army veteran. Divorced from Linda; no children. Kept the lake house in the divorce.

state_transitions.change: Dies in bed from undiagnosed hypertrophic cardiomyopathy aggravated by alcohol, sildenafil, and hot-tub heat.

characters.availability: Alive through Friday night; dead before Saturday dawn. Present later only in memory, will, and RT document.

characters.physical_details: Five-ten, thin, wire-rimmed glasses, careful grooming.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Best friend for twenty-five years; Martin was Paul's best man.
key_relationships.tommy_renaldi: Friendly for years; by chapter 17 learns Martin had quietly managed Tommy since the Ray Toomey fire.
key_relationships.joel_beckman: Becomes Paul's investigative ally after Cliff's death.

characters.occupation_status: Claims adjuster in Minneapolis. Married to Anne; two teenagers.

state_transitions.change: Finds Martin on his left side facing the window, performs CPR, then repositions the body and wipes touched surfaces.
Finds Martin's will and learns Tommy inherits the lake house.
Reads the RT document with Joel and identifies Tommy's motive.
Confronts Tommy with the will and RT document, hears Tommy's account of the fire and Cliff's murder, and sets a deadline.

characters.physical_details: Five-nine, soft in the middle, expressive face, podcast cap.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Longtime league friend; leaves the 8:47 PM Friday voicemail that later terrifies him.
key_relationships.doug_whitfield: Smoking buddy and basement roommate for the weekend.

characters.occupation_status: Fantasy football podcaster ('Griggs on the Grid'); about $400/month. Girlfriend Mia lives in Minneapolis.

state_transitions.change: Leaves the 8:47 PM Friday voicemail about Martin's draft and finds Martin just after 2 AM Saturday.
Enters Martin's room and deletes the voicemail from Martin's phone.
Accidentally reveals the deleted voicemail to Mallick.
Turns over dinner audio capturing Cliff setting the 10:30 PM dock meeting with Tommy.

characters.physical_details: Six-two, athletic, crew cut graying at the temples, large hands, pale burn scar on left forearm.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Protected by Martin for twenty-two years after the Ray Toomey fire; inherits the lake house.
key_relationships.cliff_haywood: Photographs the RT document, blackmails Tommy with it, and is killed by Tommy.
key_relationships.paul_decker: Paul becomes the friend who stops protecting him.

characters.occupation_status: High-school football coach and PE teacher in Apple Valley; Eastview program. Married to Denise; three children.

state_transitions.change: Removes the remaining sildenafil from Martin's room and flushes it.
Learns Cliff photographed and backed up the RT document and is pressuring him over Martin's debt.
Shoves Cliff at the dock; Cliff slips, hits his right temple, resurfaces, and Tommy drowns him before staging the body with Cliff's loafers.
Learns that Martin left him the lake house.
Privately tells Paul he caused the Ray Toomey fire and killed Cliff after the RT document pressure.
Confesses to Cliff's murder and is arrested.

characters.availability: Present through chapter 18; arrested Wednesday at dawn.

characters.physical_details: Five-eleven, silver hair, permanent tan, pressed polos, chinos, leather loafers indoors.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Martin owed him $40,000 from the failed Roseville duplex deal.
key_relationships.tommy_renaldi: Uses RT-document photos to pressure Tommy into backing the debt claim.
key_relationships.paul_decker: Expects Paul and the group to treat Martin's debt as real.

characters.occupation_status: Real estate developer. Business failing: three projects stalled, one note defaulting this week, two more due by October. Twice divorced.

state_transitions.change: Enters Martin's study after the death and realizes the safe is accessible.
Opens the safe, finds the RT document, and photographs it.
Uses the RT document to pressure Tommy over Martin's $40,000 debt.
Killed at the dock after striking his right temple and being held under; staged in the wrong shoes.

characters.physical_details: Six feet, gaunt after a twenty-seven-pound weight loss, bags under his eyes, hoodies and sweats.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Martin noticed Doug's decline and insisted he come after a July call about his empty fridge.
key_relationships.joel_beckman: Shares the quiet observational role in the late-book investigation.

characters.occupation_status: Pharmaceutical sales rep. Recently divorced from Steph; on escitalopram and lorazepam; living alone in Richfield.

state_transitions.change: Removes his prescription bottles from the shared upstairs bathroom and hides them in his duffel.
Finds Cliff's body in the lake.

characters.physical_details: Five-ten, lean, quick-eyed, looks younger than forty-seven.

key_relationships.martin_kessler: Former fraternity friend; came after learning Martin had been sleeping with Karen.
key_relationships.karen_beckman: Ex-wife; Joel is angry at Martin, not Karen.
key_relationships.paul_decker: Becomes Paul's ally in reading the RT document and identifying Tommy's motive.

characters.occupation_status: Public defender in Hennepin County. Divorced from Karen; daughter Ellie attends the University of Wisconsin.

state_transitions.change: Becomes the apparent suspect because of the Thursday-night fight with Martin.
Tells the group about Martin's affair with Karen.
Reads the RT document with Paul and identifies Tommy as Cliff's likely killer.

characters.physical_details: Five-six, stocky, gray-streaked bun, reading glasses on a beaded chain, blazer over a polo.

key_relationships.all_guests: Investigating authority over the draft group.

characters.occupation_status: Becker County detective in Detroit Lakes. Widowed; lives alone with two cats.

state_transitions.change: Arrives with the casserole, planted mistakes, and the folksy interview strategy.
Concludes Martin died naturally and that five separate people tampered independently.
Returns for Cliff's homicide, gets dinner audio placing Cliff and Tommy at a 10:30 PM dock meeting, and drops the folksy act.
Gets Tommy's confession and arrests him.

characters.availability: On-page from Saturday morning through Wednesday arrest.

timeline.story_start: Thursday in September 2024, arrival day for the twenty-fifth annual fantasy-football draft weekend.
timeline.estimated_span: About six days, Thursday arrival through early Wednesday arrest.

seasonal_track.season: September in northern Minnesota: warm afternoons, cool nights, turning leaves, golden lake light.
Same week but colder at night: dew-slick boards, visible breath, and an October edge off the lake.

key_events.event: Arrival, Dent sandwiches, and the annual draft
Lake day, hot tub, Tommy's sildenafil joke; Martin takes one pill late Friday
Nate leaves Martin a death-language voicemail
Nate finds Martin dead; Paul performs CPR
Five tamperings occur: body/surfaces, voicemail, safe, prescription bottles, remaining pills
Tommy calls 911; deputies and Mallick arrive
Cliff opens the safe, finds the RT document, and photographs it
Joel tells the group about Martin and Karen
Mallick concludes Martin's death was natural and the scene was altered by five people
Paul finds the will and learns Tommy inherits the lake house
Cliff uses the RT document to pressure Tommy over Martin's debt
Dinner audio captures Cliff setting a 10:30 PM dock meeting with Tommy
Tommy kills Cliff at the dock and stages the body with Cliff's loafers
Joel and Paul read the RT document and identify Tommy's motive
Paul confronts Tommy with the will and RT document; Tommy privately admits the fire and Cliff's killing before dawn
Tommy confesses to Mallick and is arrested

geography.primary_setting: Martin Kessler's A-frame lake house on Elbow Lake in rural Becker County, Minnesota.

key_locations.name: Great room and kitchen
key_locations.details: Vaulted main room with stone fireplace, open kitchen, and the draft board on the wall.

key_locations.name: Upper floor
key_locations.details: Master bedroom, guest rooms, and the shared bathroom; the third stair from the top creaks.

key_locations.name: Green room off the hall
key_locations.details: First-floor guest room by the slider and kitchen; Martin usually puts Tommy here and keeps his spare supplies there.

key_locations.name: Front hall half bath
key_locations.details: Small downstairs bath off the hall; Tommy flushes the remaining sildenafil here.

key_locations.name: Basement
key_locations.details: Finished lower level with two pull-out sofas, bathroom, laundry, and small lake windows; Nate and Doug sleep here.

key_locations.name: Martin's study
key_locations.details: Downstairs office two doors past the half bath, with a thumb-latch door and a wall safe behind a lake print.

key_locations.name: Deck and hot tub
key_locations.details: Wraparound lake-side deck with Adirondacks, grill, propane heater, and six-person hot tub.

key_locations.name: Firepit and dock path
key_locations.details: Stone-ringed firepit between house and lake; an 80-yard path leads through pine and birch to the dock.

key_locations.name: The dock
key_locations.details: Thirty-foot dock with a bench and swim platform; water beside it is about 3 feet deep.

distances: Quarter-mile gravel driveway through pines before the house comes into view
Lake house to Dent, Minnesota: about 15 minutes by car
Lake house to Detroit Lakes: 26 to 30 minutes by car
Deck to dock: about an 80-yard stone path through pine and birch
Dock length: about 30 feet; water beside it is about 3 feet deep

world_rules: Contemporary realistic fiction set in northern Minnesota.
Becker County handles the initial response; the BCA joins once the second death looks like homicide.
Cell service is best on the deck, drive, and road; worse inside, the basement, and the dock.
September weather turns quickly from warm afternoons to cold nights; dew makes deck and dock slick after dark.
Seven-man keeper league; owners keep up to three players; commissioner resolves disputes at the lake house.

power_structure.mechanism: The RT document and twenty-two years of silence over Ray Toomey's death.
RT-document photos plus Tommy's fear that the Ray Toomey fire would surface.
County authority plus an interview strategy built around planted errors and guest corrections.
By chapter 16 they know Cliff found the RT document and Tommy had motive.

objects.name: RT fire document
objects.owner: Martin Kessler's estate; police evidence by chapter 18
objects.origin: Martin's handwritten August 17, 2002 account of the Wolf Creek Road fire that killed caretaker Ray Toomey.
objects.status: In Martin's safe for twenty-two years; Cliff photographs it Sunday, uses it Monday, Joel and Paul read it Tuesday, and Mallick later recovers it.

objects.name: Wall safe
objects.origin: Wall-mounted safe behind the lake print in the study.
objects.status: Usually left one tick off locked; holds records, the will, cash, and the RT envelope.

objects.name: Martin's will
objects.origin: Last Will and Testament, dated March 14, 2023.
objects.status: Found by Paul in chapter 11; leaves the house to Tommy, investments to Carol, the car to a cousin, and money to U of M athletics.

objects.name: Martin's phone
objects.origin: Personal smartphone kept on the nightstand.
objects.status: Receives Nate's 8:47 PM Friday voicemail; Nate deletes it around 3:15 AM Saturday; taken into evidence that morning.

objects.name: Sildenafil sample pack
objects.origin: Four-pill sample pack Tommy bought from a gym contact as a joke gift.
objects.status: Martin takes one pill late Friday; Tommy flushes the remaining three and keeps the empty pack.

objects.name: Doug's prescription bottles
objects.status: Moved from the shared upstairs bathroom to Doug's duffel before police arrive.

objects.name: Nate's recording setup
objects.origin: Phone and clip-on mic for podcast audio.
objects.status: Captures Thursday draft audio and Monday dinner audio, including Cliff setting the 10:30 PM dock meeting; later given to Mallick.

objects.name: Cliff's phone
objects.status: Contains RT-document photos and leverage texts; missing from Cliff's body, then recovered into evidence.

objects.name: Draft board
objects.origin: Physical whiteboard hung each year in the great room.
objects.status: Seven-man board with Martin in the final slot; remains on the wall.

objects.name: Cliff's loafers
objects.origin: Indoor leather house shoes, not dock shoes.
objects.status: Tommy puts them on Cliff's body after the murder; the wrong shoes expose staging.

objects.name: Green bean casserole
objects.origin: Pyrex dish Mallick brings Saturday morning.
objects.status: Untouched in Martin's refrigerator until Mallick leaves with it.

debts.deadline: No formal note; Cliff needs repayment urgently because one project note defaults this week and two more are due in October
debts.status: Supported by an unsigned handwritten note in Martin's safe; unresolved after Cliff's death and Tommy's arrest

knowledge_state.knows: Tommy caused the 2002 Ray Toomey fire and Martin buried the truth
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone but Tommy until the RT document surfaces

knowledge_state.knows: He caused the Ray Toomey fire and Martin kept an RT account in the safe
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Everyone until Cliff finds the RT document

knowledge_state.learned_in: One month before the draft, at Ellie's move-in

knowledge_state.knows: The RT document ties Tommy to Ray Toomey's death and gives Cliff leverage
knowledge_state.hidden_from: All but Tommy until phone extraction after Cliff's death

knowledge_state.knows: Martin left the lake house to Tommy
The RT document's contents and Tommy's likely motive for killing Cliff
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Group until chapter_17; Mallick until chapter_18

knowledge_state.knows: The RT document's contents and Tommy's likely motive for killing Cliff
knowledge_state.hidden_from: Group until chapter_17; Mallick until chapter_18

knowledge_state.knows: Cliff photographed the RT document and backed the leverage up elsewhere
Martin left him the lake house and contents
He deleted the voicemail from Martin's phone
He removed his prescription bottles before police arrived
Martin died naturally, five people tampered independently, and Tommy killed Cliff

environmental_constants: Water is audible from the deck, dock path, and basement.
Pine-paneled walls smell of wood smoke, lake damp, and Martin's Lagavulin.
The third stair from the top creaks.
The old refrigerator cycles about every 40 minutes and can be heard through most of the house.
September light runs gold by afternoon; nights turn cold enough for dew and breath.
Cars on the quarter-mile gravel driveway are heard before they are seen.
The dock path disappears into trees quickly, so the dock is hard to see after dark.
